creator: se@FreeBSD.org
text:
It works for me with fstyp from -CURRENT and from 13.3-RC1 on amd64.

I get (identical output on both FreeBSD versions):

$ file -s /dev/md0s1
/dev/md0s1: DOS/MBR boot sector, code offset 0x3c+2, OEM-ID "MSWIN4.1", sectors/cluster 8, root entries 512, Media descriptor 0xf8, sectors/FAT 129, sectors/track 1, heads 1, hidden sectors 1, sectors 262143 (volumes > 32 MB), serial number 0xb37e2, label: "RPI-RP2    ", FAT (16 bit)

$ fstyp /dev/md0s1
msdosfs

If the problem persists on your system, you may want to run fstyp (with debug info) in a debugger or add print statements at various locations in:

/usr/src/usr.sbin/fstyp/msdosfs.c

I have checked the sources of FreeBSD-13.1 for differences in fstyp/msdosfs.c and there were none that effect the detection. But there were differences between 13.0 and 13.1, especially for RPi images:

+       /* Special case for Raspberry Pi Pico bootloader. */
+       if (sector0[510] == 0 && sector0[511] == 0 &&
+           sector0[0] == 0xeb && sector0[1] == 0x3c && sector0[2] == 0x90)
+               return (true);

But this special case should be in your version of fstyp, if you are on FreeBSD-13.1 (as indicated in the information your provide in the PR).
